SHADYA suspiciously fast local drop.
SetupPrivacySecurityAbout

Setup Guide

Get SHADY running in under 2 minutes.

Quick Install

Install

brew install node && npm install -g @shady/tui

Run

shady
1

Install the TUI

Install SHADY globally with npm. Requires Node.js 18 or later.

npmnpm install -g @shady/tui
yarnyarn global add @shady/tui
pnpmpnpm add -g @shady/tui

You can also build from source: git clone https://github.com/shady-app/shady && cd shady/apps/tui && npm install && npm run dev

2

Start the Receiver

Run shady in your terminal. A QR code and pairing code will appear.

startshady
offline modeshady --offline
custom nameshady --name "My Computer"

The receiver registers itself with the discovery service and starts waiting for connections.

3

Open the Web App on Your Phone

On the sending device, open this website. Your computer should appear in the Nearby Receivers list.

urlhttps://shady-app.vercel.app

Both devices must be on the same network for nearby discovery to work. Alternatively, scan the QR code from the terminal.

4

Approve the Connection

When someone tries to connect, a pairing request appears in your terminal. A 6-digit code is shown on both devices.

Verify that the code on your phone matches the code in your terminal. Press A to accept, R to reject.

5

Send Files

Once approved, a direct WebRTC connection is established. Drop files in the browser — they transfer straight to your computer.

Files are encrypted end-to-end. Nothing passes through any server. Transfer speed is limited only by your local network.

6

Files Are Saved

Received files are verified with SHA-256 and saved to ~/Downloads/SHADY by default.

default path~/Downloads/SHADY
custom pathshady --output /path/to/folder

Check the Activity log in the TUI for transfer status. Incomplete transfers can be resumed.

TUI Keyboard Shortcuts

AAccept connection
RReject connection
SSend file
FSend folder
CSend clipboard
QRegenerate QR code
OToggle online/offline
NRename receiver
PPause transfer
XCancel transfer
DNetwork diagnostics
?Show help
Ctrl+CExit safely

Troubleshooting

Receiver not showing up

Make sure both devices are on the same Wi-Fi network. If using a VPN, disconnect it — VPNs block local discovery.

QR code not scanning

Ensure the terminal supports QR codes. Most modern terminals do. Try the manual pairing code instead.

Connection fails after approval

Your network may be blocking WebRTC. Try offline mode: run shady --offline on the same Wi-Fi.

Transfer is slow

Check your Wi-Fi signal. For best speed, use a wired connection or 5 GHz Wi-Fi. Large files are chunked at 256 KiB.

Run diagnostics

Press D in the TUI to run network diagnostics and see what's working.

SHADY transfers are end-to-end encrypted via WebRTC DTLS. Files never pass through any server. Read the security model for details.

SHADY v0.1.0Files transfer directly. No server storage.P2P
